title: lathraea japonica var. miqueliana
intro: Lathraea japonica var. miqueliana, commonly known as the Japanese toothwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the genus Lathraea. It is known for its unique appearance, with yellowish-green flowers and a reddish-brown root system. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Lathraea japonica var. miqueliana include relief from respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, and it is often used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions for these purposes.
